WhyUseful: Shows the velocity COMMANDS being sent TO the motors — essential to see what the controller is telling the wheels to do
HowToUseInGraceWeb: Gauge charts (already in Dashboard) — shows [right left] wheel target speeds
Format: -180 to +180 RPM per wheel as float array [right left]

WhyUseful: Shows the ACTUAL velocity from wheel encoders — essential to compare what was commanded vs what actually happened
HowToUseInGraceWeb: Gauge charts (already in Dashboard) — shows actual encoder feedback per wheel
Format: velocity[] and position[] arrays per joint name

WhyUseful: Robot's computed position (x y theta) and velocity from wheel odometry — shows where the robot thinks it is and how fast it's moving
HowToUseInGraceWeb: Dashboard: live position XY display and linear/angular velocity readout. Viz: draw robot trail on a mini-map

WhyUseful: The high-level velocity command (linear + angular) being sent to the robot — useful to see what the controller is requesting before differential kinematics
HowToUseInGraceWeb: Dashboard: show commanded linear.x (m/s) and angular.z (rad/s) as number readouts

WhyUseful: Robot orientation and angular velocity from IMU — can animate the 3D model to show real-time tilt/roll/pitch of the robot
HowToUseInGraceWeb: Viz: rotate the 3D STL model in real-time to match actual robot pose. Dashboard: show roll/pitch/yaw numbers

WhyUseful: 360° laser scan — already used in Map page but could also show a mini radar view in dashboard
HowToUseInGraceWeb: Dashboard: mini radar/polar plot showing obstacles around the robot. Already in Map page as point overlay
Format: ranges[] array with angle_min angle_max angle_increment

WhyUseful: Emergency stop status — critical to show whether the robot is locked or free to move
HowToUseInGraceWeb: Dashboard: big red/green indicator showing LOCKED vs UNLOCKED status

WhyUseful: Joystick velocity input — shows if someone is controlling via joystick (priority 99)
HowToUseInGraceWeb: Dashboard: small indicator showing active control source (Joystick vs Keyboard vs Nav2)

WhyUseful: Keyboard velocity input — shows if keyboard teleop is active (priority 90)
Nav2 autonomous velocity — shows if the robot is navigating autonomously (priority 80)
0.08255m — needed to compute actual speed from rad/s to m/s if converting units
0.45468m — needed to understand turning radius and differential kinematics
0.7 m/s — useful for gauge scaling and speed limit indicators
HowToUseInGraceWeb: Dashboard: max speed reference line on gauges
